rationale: A anchors the right REST entity and the closest resource test, which is what an engineer needs for request-validation work. B uses versioned URLs, but its test anchors are mostly peripheral and it omits the main resource class, making it less useful and more likely to mislead. Both are somewhat speculative on exact validation branches, but A is closer to the relevant source surface.

A: Docs anchor is only the source-tree `docs/connect.html`, not an explicit version-pinned public doc URL.
Does not name a dedicated `CreateConnectorRequestTest` if one exists, so test anchoring is slightly incomplete.
B: Test anchors are largely unrelated to `CreateConnectorRequest` validation and miss `ConnectorsResourceTest`.
No resource-class anchor like `ConnectorsResource.java`, so the validation path is under-specified.
`repo://...@4.2` and `kafka.org/42` may be too loosely version-pinned for a 4.2.0-specific question.
